The goal is for every important piece of music work to have a home.
• Find It search results for prompt history.

• Right-click a lyric line → add meaning note.
• Right-click a manual word → create deeper explanation page.
• Right-click a metadata record → add relationship.
• Right-click a project card → add project note.

• Save current Find It result as a path.
• Show saved paths in Find It suggestions.
• Use saved paths as onboarding and help routes.
Navigation and metadata sources exist. More source types are planned.
Future AI systems should create versions instead of overwriting work.
The app should help preserve evolution instead of replacing history.
Version awareness is expected to become a major future project feature.
, the player, metadata records, and future AI tools.

• Future AI regeneration tasks for one stem instead of the full song.
• Metadata records linked to stem files.
• Notes attached to specific stems and song moments.
• Player controls for isolated stem playback.
• Stem groups for vocals, drums, bass, instruments, and effects.
• Stem upload and organization inside projects.
• Basic title, artist, source, and project.
• Better natural-language matching beyond simple text search.
• Deeper route explanations for nested pages.
• Search result filters by Navigation, Metadata, Manual, and Project.
What the Global Player is now
• Future stem and waveform navigation tools.
• Metadata record links from playing tracks.
• Stronger track details connected to the player.
A future workflow might look like:
• Build future AI systems on structured metadata foundations.
• Create reusable systems instead of one-off hacks.
• Make navigation understandable for ADD-style workflows.
• Split oversized files before architecture becomes unstable.
• Better connection between project songs and the player.
• Clear project tree / child page structure.
• Future AI generation history inside each project.
, then branches into working tools and manual explanations.

A Few Questions To Help You Get Started
A blank Island is full of possibility.
A blank Island is not unfinished.
An Island is a place where ideas are allowed to grow.
An Island is not a website.
Build as much or as little as you like.
Every Island begins as a blank space.
Give your Island a name. You can change it at any time.

I Already Know What I Want To Build
I Have No Idea Where To Start

Many creators arrive with a vision already forming in their minds.
Many great Islands begin that way.
Now show us what you can imagine.
Some members create a single page. Others create entire worlds.
Why Is My Island So Empty?
You are free to build something nobody has ever seen before.
You are free to change your mind.
You are free to start over.
You decide what remains private and what gets shared.
You may fill out as much or as little of your Island as you wish.
Your Island is your personal space inside The Muzes Garden.
